methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Arondite is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Will Blyth and Rob Underhill that has raised $12M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public peers in the same sector, including Seed and Series A stage companies such as Tenna Systems ($60M), Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Stark Defence ($500M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $12M raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $30M. Peer-median analysis across the four comparable companies produces a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. The peer set skews toward Series A valuations, which elevates this estimate relative to Arondite's current stage.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $26M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $128M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: the stage-progression method anchors conservatively to capital deployed, while the peer-median method incorporates later-stage comparables that may not reflect Arondite's current risk profile. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, IP defensibility within Defense & Security, and customer concentration. Resolution of these factors through near-term operational milestones would materially narrow the range.
